login.newPasswordRequired: Your account requires a new password before continuing.

memory.botMemoryDesc: Bot operating manual — identity, personality, rules, and notes (CLAUDE.md)

settings.triggerDesc: The regex pattern that activates this bot in group chats. Messages matching this pattern will be forwarded to the agent.
settings.dangerDesc: Deleting this bot will permanently remove all its channels, conversations, tasks, memory, and session data.
settings.deleteBotConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.

telegram.label: Telegram
telegram.guideTitle: How to get your Telegram Bot Token
telegram.autoWebhook: The webhook will be auto-registered when you connect.

discord.label: Discord
discord.step1desc: Go to <0>Discord Developer Portal</0> → <1>New Application</1> → give it a name → <2>Create</2>.
discord.step2desc: Left menu → <0>Bot</0> → click <1>Reset Token</1> → copy it immediately (you can only see it once).
discord.step3desc: On the same <0>Bot</0> page, scroll to <1>Privileged Gateway Intents</1> and enable:
discord.step3intent1: <0>Message Content Intent</0> — required to read message text
discord.step3intent2: <0>Server Members Intent</0> — optional, for member display names
discord.step4desc: Left menu → <0>General Information</0> → copy the <1>Public Key</1> (64-character hex string).
discord.step5final: Copy the generated URL → open in browser → select your server → <0>Authorize</0>.
discord.fillFields: Fill in both fields below, then click Connect.
discord.connectedDesc: Credentials verified and stored. Complete the remaining steps:
discord.afterStep1desc: In Discord Developer Portal → your app → <0>General Information</0> → paste this into <1>Interactions Endpoint URL</1>:
discord.afterStep1verify: Discord will send a verification ping — you should see a success message.
discord.afterStep2desc: To use the bot in <0>private channels</0>, you must add it explicitly:
discord.afterStep2item1: Open the private channel → click the channel name (gear icon) → <0>Permissions</0>
discord.afterStep2item2: Click <0>+</0> to add members/roles → search for your bot name
discord.afterStep2item3: Ensure <0>View Channel</0>, <1>Send Messages</1>, and <2>Read Message History</2> are enabled
discord.afterStep2note: Public channels work automatically — the bot can see any channel it has access to.
discord.afterStep3desc: The bot supports multiple interaction methods:
discord.afterStep3method2: <0>Slash commands</0> — type <1>/ask</1> to send a private question, <2>/status</2> for bot info, <3>/help</3> for usage guide
discord.afterStep3method3: <0>Direct messages</0> — DM the bot directly for private conversations
discord.afterStep3note: Replies use rich embeds with response time. Slash command replies are private by default.

slack.label: Slack
slack.step1desc: Go to <0>api.slack.com/apps</0> → <1>Create New App</1> → <2>From scratch</2> → name it and select your workspace.
slack.step2desc: Left menu → <0>OAuth & Permissions</0> → scroll to <1>Bot Token Scopes</1> → add:
slack.step2scope7: <0>files:read</0> — Read file attachments sent by users
slack.step2scopeNote: The <0>files:read</0> and <1>files:write</1> scopes are needed for sending and receiving file attachments.
slack.step3desc: Left menu → <0>App Home</0> → scroll to <1>Show Tabs</1>:
slack.step3item2: Check <0>"Allow users to send Slash commands and messages from the messages tab"</0>
slack.step3note: This enables users to DM the bot directly.
slack.step4desc: Left menu → <0>OAuth & Permissions</0> → click <1>Install to Workspace</1> → Authorize.
slack.step5desc: After installing, copy the <0>Bot User OAuth Token</0> (starts with <1>xoxb-</1>). Paste it below.
slack.step6desc: Left menu → <0>Basic Information</0> → scroll to <1>App Credentials</1> → click <2>Show</2> next to <3>Signing Secret</3>. Paste it below.
slack.fillBoth: Fill in both fields below, then click Connect. After connecting, you'll get instructions for the final step (Event Subscriptions).
slack.connectedDesc: Credentials verified and stored. Now complete the final step to receive messages:
slack.afterStep1desc: In your Slack App settings → left menu → <0>Event Subscriptions</0> → toggle <1>Enable Events</1> to ON.
slack.afterStep2desc: Paste this URL into the <0>Request URL</0> field:
slack.afterStep2verify: Slack will send a verification request — you should see a green checkmark ✓
slack.afterStep3desc: Below the Request URL, expand <0>Subscribe to bot events</0> → click <1>Add Bot User Event</1> → add:
slack.afterStep4desc: Click <0>Save Changes</0>. Then go to <1>OAuth & Permissions</1> → <2>Reinstall to Workspace</2> (required after adding scopes or event subscriptions). Finally, invite the bot to a channel: type <3>/invite @YourBotName</3>
slack.afterDone: Once done, send a message mentioning your bot (e.g., <0>@BotName hello</0>) and it will respond!

feishu.label: Feishu
feishu.step1desc: 打开 <0>飞书开放平台 (open.feishu.cn)</0> → <1>创建自建应用</1>
feishu.step2title: 获取 App ID 和 App Secret
feishu.step2desc: 在应用的 <0>凭证与基础信息</0> 页面获取 App ID 和 App Secret
feishu.step3title: 获取 Encrypt Key 和 Verification Token
feishu.step3desc: 在「<0>事件与回调</0>」中获取 Encrypt Key 和 Verification Token
feishu.step4desc: 在「<0>权限管理</0>」中申请以下权限：
feishu.step4batchImport: 推荐使用「批量开通」导入以下 JSON，或手动逐个申请：
feishu.step4expandJson: 点击展开批量导入 JSON
feishu.step4imSendAsBot: <0>im:message:send_as_bot</0> — 以机器人身份发送消息
feishu.step4imGroupAt: <0>im:message.group_at_msg:readonly</0> — 读取群聊 @机器人 消息
feishu.step4imGroupMsg: <0>im:message.group_msg</0> — <1>获取群聊中所有消息（敏感权限，需审批）</1>
feishu.step4imResource: <0>im:resource</0> — 读取消息中的资源文件
feishu.step4docsTitle: 文档与知识库（启用飞书文档工具时需要）
feishu.step4docsItems: docs:doc, docs:doc:readonly, docx:document, docx:document:readonly, docx:document.block:convert, wiki:wiki, wiki:wiki:readonly
feishu.step4driveTitle: 云盘与权限管理（启用相应工具时需要）
feishu.step5desc: 创建并发布一个应用版本，等待管理员审核通过
feishu.fillCredentials: 填写以下凭证信息，然后点击「Connect Channel」。WebSocket 连接将自动建立，无需额外配置回调地址。
feishu.connectedDesc: 凭证验证通过并已安全存储。WebSocket 长连接已自动建立，无需额外配置回调地址。
feishu.connectedConfirm: 请确认飞书开放平台已完成以下配置：
feishu.afterStep1desc: 在「<0>事件与回调</0>」的事件订阅设置中，选择「<1>使用长连接接收事件</1>」方式
feishu.afterStep3desc: 在「<0>应用能力</0>」中启用「<1>机器人</1>」能力
feishu.afterStep4desc: 将机器人添加到目标群组
feishu.afterStep5desc: 在群组中 @机器人 或私聊发消息进行测试

memoryEditor.botDesc: Bot operating manual — identity, personality, rules, and notes (CLAUDE.md)
memoryEditor.sharedPlaceholder:
# Shared Memory

Enter instructions and context shared across all your bots...
memoryEditor.groupMemoryHint: This conversation has no independent memory yet. The bot will use its bot-level memory by default. Group memory is created automatically when the agent saves conversation-specific context, or you can add it manually below.

providers.title: Model Providers
providers.description: Manage AI model providers available to bots. Configure Bedrock or Anthropic-compatible API endpoints.
providers.noProviders: No providers configured. Add one to get started.
providers.baseUrlHint: Leave empty to use default Anthropic API endpoint.
providers.apiKeyPlaceholderBedrock: Optional — uses IAM role by default
providers.noModels: No models selected. Select presets above or add custom model IDs.
providers.setDefaultHint: New bots will use this provider unless overridden.
providers.deleteConfirm: Delete this model provider? Bots using it will fall back to the default provider.

credentials.title: API Credential Proxy Rules
credentials.description: Configure credentials injected into agent API calls. Keys are never exposed to the agent — they are injected by a secure local proxy.
credentials.noRules: No credential rules configured. Use a preset below or add a custom rule.
credentials.name: Name

users.suspendConfirm: Suspend this user? They will lose access to the platform.
users.deleteConfirm: Delete this user? This action cannot be undone.
